scheme_name: Marriage Grant for the Marriage of Daughters of Widows of Ex-servicemen (other than War Widows)
page_title: Marriage Grant for the Marriage of Daughters of Widows of Ex-servicemen (other than War Widows)

details: The "Marriage Grant for the Marriage of Daughters of Widows of Ex-servicemen (other than War Widows)" scheme is implemented by the Sainik and Ardh Sainik Welfare Department, Government of Haryana. Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹11,000/- is provided to the widows of ex-servicemen (other than war widows) for the marriage of their daughters, for up to two daughters who are at least 18 years old.
benefits: Financial assistance of ₹11,000/- is given to the widows of ex-servicemen (other than war widows) for the marriage of their daughters.
eligibility: The applicant should be a resident of Haryana.
The applicant should be the widow of an ex-serviceman who did not die in the war (other than a war widow).
The daughter should be at least 18 years old.
The widow can receive the benefit for up to two of her daughters.
The widow should not have remarried. If remarried, she should only have married the real brother of her late husband.
The entire verification of claimants will be carried out at the level of the Secretaries of Zila Sainik Boards as hither-to-fore.

online: Registration Process on Antyodaya-SARAL Portal:
Step 1:
The eligible applicant may apply online through the Official Portal-
Antyodaya-SARAL Portal
Step 2:
If the applicant is not registered on the portal, he/she gets registered there.
Step 3:
For registration, click “
New User
” under ‘Sign In Here’ and fill in all the mandatory details i.e. Name, Email ID, Mobile Number, Password & State. Click on ‘Submit’.
Step 4:
Verify your mobile number and email ID through the received OTP and complete the registration process.
Login to apply for the scheme:
To avail the benefits of the scheme, visit the Official
and log in through the email ID used while registering.
Enter Password & Captcha and click on ‘Login’.
After successful login, click on ‘Apply for Services’ and then ‘View all Available Services’.
Now, you can search for the scheme and download the pre-defined documents form/template and you will need to upload the filled-in/signed form/template in the 'Attach Enclosures' screen.
Step 5:

Click on ‘Proceed to Apply’ to fill out the application form. Enter your Parivar Pehchan Patra number- Family ID and click on “Click here to Fetch Family Data” which displays the family members registered under the given Family ID.
Step 6:
Select the beneficiary/applicant name and enter the OTP that is being sent to the selected family member for verification. Click on “Click to Verify”
Step 7:
Fill in all the mandatory details and upload all the mandatory documents.
Step 8:
Preview the application form and click on ‘Submit’.
Tracking of Application:
Applicants can track their applications through the Official
by entering their Department Name, Scheme Name, and Application Reference ID.

documents_required: Identity proof of widow of ex-serviceman
Copy of Bank Passbook linked with Aadhaar Card
Applicant’s Photo ID Proof (Ration Card/Driving Licence/ Voter ID Card/PAN Card etc.)
Age Proof- Matric Exam Certificate for Date of Birth verification (attested)

faqs.question: What is the primary benefit of this scheme?
faqs.answer: The primary benefit is financial assistance of ₹11,000/- for the marriage of up to two daughters of widows of ex-servicemen (other than war widows).

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The widow of an ex-serviceman who did not die in the war, is a resident of Haryana, and whose daughter(s) are at least 18 years old.

faqs.question: Does the widow need to be a resident of Haryana to be eligible?
faqs.answer: Yes, the widow must be a resident of Haryana.

faqs.question: Can a widow who has remarried apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, but only if she has remarried the real brother of her late husband.

faqs.question: What is the age requirement for the daughters to receive this grant?
faqs.answer: The daughter should be at least 18 years old.

faqs.question: How many daughters can receive this grant under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The widow can receive the benefit for up to two of her daughters.

faqs.question: How can eligible applicants apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Eligible applicants can apply online through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal at https://saralharyana.gov.in/

faqs.question: How can applicants track their application status?
faqs.answer: Applicants can track their applications through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal by entering their Department Name, Scheme Name, and Application Reference ID at https://status.saralharyana.nic.in/
